summary: Frontend testing completed for Blog section with tag filtering system. All 12 requested features verified working correctly.
passed_tests: ✓ BlogPage loads correctly with hero, tag filter section, and article grid (data-testid='blog-page')
✓ Tag category buttons (Istituzionali, Geografici, Gastronomici, Ispirazione e Trend) all visible and functional
✓ Category expand/collapse works - clicking Istituzionali shows 5 tag pills (#TenutaLeone, #FirmaDAutore, etc.)
✓ Clicking a tag pill filters articles and shows active filter indicator with article count ('Filtro attivo: #TenutaLeone 1 articolo')
✓ 'Mostra tutti' button appears when filter is active and clears the filter correctly
✓ Accordion behavior works - clicking different category collapses previous and expands new one
✓ Blog article cards show tags (first 4 + '+13' count of remaining)
✓ BlogArticlePage loads correctly with article content and 17 tags section at bottom
✓ Tags on article page are clickable links with correct href (/blog?tag=TagName)
✓ Blog page correctly reads tag query parameter from URL and applies filter on load
✓ Mobile responsive: tag categories stack vertically (full width), tag pills wrap properly
✓ BlogPreview component on homepage renders correctly with article title and image
✓ Navigation from homepage blog preview to blog article works correctly
critical_code_review_comments: Code is well-structured with proper data-testid attributes for testing
Tag filtering uses useSearchParams correctly for URL-based state management
useMemo used appropriately for filtering performance
Mobile responsive CSS properly implemented with flex-direction: column for categories
context_for_next_testing_agent: Blog section fully tested: 1) BlogPage with tag filter system ✓ 2) 4 tag categories with accordion behavior ✓ 3) Tag filtering with URL params ✓ 4) BlogArticlePage with clickable tags ✓ 5) BlogPreview on homepage ✓ 6) Mobile responsive ✓. Currently only 1 dummy article exists in blogData.js.

mocked_apis_list: Blog data is static from blogData.js (no backend API)
